brand.name: Nurlan

seo.defaultDescription: A storytelling social network for the most interesting human experiences online. Real people, real moments, anonymously if you want.

rules.title: Before you publish
rules.subtitle: A few simple rules for respectful and enjoyable communication on the site.
rules.rule1: No hate speech, slurs, or attacks on individuals or groups.
rules.rule2: No sexual content involving minors, ever. This is a hard ban.
rules.rule3: No personal information (doxxing), threats, or harassment.
rules.rule4: No spam, scams, or commercial promotion.
rules.rule5: No graphic shock content for its own sake.
rules.footer: Stories that break these rules can be removed without warning, and repeat offenders lose their accounts.
rules.agreeLabel: I've read these and I'll follow them.

footer.tagline: Stories that hit. Real people. Real moments.

about.title: We're building the place real stories live.
about.lede: Nurlan is a home for the kind of stories you'd usually only hear at 2am with a friend. Honest, weird, painful, hilarious — the ones that get cut from polished feeds.
about.section1Body: Social media flattened storytelling into bite-sized hits. We wanted something with texture again — long enough to feel something, short enough to finish on the bus.
about.section2Body: A typing room, a comment thread, and a feed that rewards stories people actually finish. No algorithm gaming. No ad chasing. Just stories that hit.
about.section3Body: Writers, lurkers, oversharers, people processing yesterday. Anyone with something to say and the patience to say it well.
about.value1Body: We'd rather read a rough story that's true than a clean one that isn't.
about.value2Body: No notification spam. No engagement bait. The feed earns your time.
about.value3Body: Real stories sometimes need a stage name. Post anonymously whenever you want.
about.ctaBody: Whatever it is, somebody here will recognize it.

news.title: What's new on Nurlan
news.lede: Recent releases, design updates, and what we're working on next.

items.title: Admin user moderation
items.body: Admins can now permanently remove user accounts from /account/admin/users. Two-step confirmation: type the username to match before deletion. Cascades through every story, comment, reaction and upload that user touched.

items.title: Show / hide on every password field
items.body: Tap the eye in any password input to peek at what you typed; tap again to hide. Animated icon morph and a soft press feedback.

items.title: Password reset
items.body: Forgot password is now a real flow. Enter your email, click the link in your inbox, set a new one. Doesn't leak whether the address is registered.

items.title: Rate limiting + open-redirect fix
items.body: Sign-in, signup, reset, comments, story posts and search are now throttled per IP and per user — a long way against credential stuffing and spam. Also: the OAuth callback's `next` parameter is now strictly validated so a phishing link can't bounce you off-site.

items.title: Edit your stories
items.body: Posted something with a typo? Authors can now edit their own stories — title, body, category, tags, media. Edits don't reset the published date.

items.title: Story views — counted once per reader
items.body: Stories now track unique views. One per reader, per story — refresh doesn't pad the number. Shows on every feed card.

items.title: AI signals on every story
items.body: Every new story will get a small AI-generated card: emotion, drama, viral potential, and a one-line summary that replaces the boilerplate preview. Schema's already there; wiring is up next.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Plain-English version: we collect the bare minimum to run the site, we don't sell your data, and we explain everything below.

sections.title: What we collect
sections.body: When you sign up, we store your email and any profile information you choose to add (username, display name, bio, avatar URL). When you post or comment, we store that content. We log basic technical data (timestamps, IP for rate-limiting) like every web service does.

sections.title: What we don't do
sections.body: We don't sell your data. We don't share it with advertisers. We don't run third-party tracking pixels. Analytics, if any, are first-party and aggregate.

sections.title: Anonymous posting
sections.body: You can publish stories anonymously. Your username is hidden from the public, but we keep the link internally so we can moderate abuse. Law enforcement requests are evaluated on a case-by-case basis with a strong bias toward your privacy.

sections.title: Authentication providers
sections.body: If you sign in with Twitch or Google, those services share your email and basic profile info with us per their normal OAuth flow. We don't get your password or access anything else.

sections.title: Your control
sections.body: You can edit your profile, change your email, change your password, delete individual stories or comments, or delete your entire account from /account/settings. Account deletion is immediate and cascades to all your content.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.body: We use a single auth cookie to keep you logged in. That's it. No tracking cookies.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: Questions about your data? Email khalilaliev0@gmail.com.

rulesPage.title: Community Rules
rulesPage.intro: Five rules. Read them once. They're the same ones you saw before publishing your first story.

sections.title: No hate speech
sections.body: No slurs. No targeting individuals or groups for who they are. Disagreement is fine; dehumanizing language isn't.

sections.title: Nothing involving minors sexually
sections.body: Zero tolerance. Instant ban. Reported to authorities where applicable.

sections.title: No doxxing, threats, or harassment
sections.body: Don't post other people's personal information. Don't threaten anyone. Don't pile on. Telling your story is fine; weaponizing it isn't.

sections.title: No spam or scams
sections.body: No commercial promotion, no affiliate dumps, no Nigerian-prince posts. If you want to advertise, this isn't the place.

sections.title: No shock for shock's sake
sections.body: Graphic detail is fine if the story earns it. Gore or trauma porn purely to disturb people is not.

rulesPage.enforcementBody: Admins can remove any story or comment that breaks these rules. First offense is usually a removal with a note. Repeat offenders lose their account. Hate speech and CSAM are instant bans.
rulesPage.appealTitle: If you think we got it wrong
rulesPage.appealBody: Email khalilaliev0@gmail.com and we'll look at it. Mistakes happen — we'd rather fix one than let it stand.
